children.label: A
children.text: The term “cost” means the estimated long-term cost to the Government of a direct loan or loan guarantee or modification thereof, calculated on a net present value basis, excluding administrative costs and any incidental effects on governmental receipts or outlays.

children.label: i
children.text: Loan disbursements.

children.label: ii
children.text: Repayments of principal.

children.label: iii
children.text: Payments of interest and other payments by or to the Government over the life of the loan after adjusting for estimated defaults, prepayments, fees, penalties, and other recoveries.

children.label: i
children.text: Payments by the Government to cover defaults and delinquencies, interest subsidies, or other payments.

children.label: ii
children.text: Payments to the Government, including origination and other fees, penalties, and recoveries.

children.label: D
children.text: The cost of a modification is the difference between the current estimate of the net present value of the remaining cash flows under the terms of a direct loan or loan guarantee contract, and the current estimate of the net present value of the remaining cash flows under the terms of the contract, as modified.

children.label: E
children.text: In estimating net present values, the discount rate shall be the average interest rate on marketable Treasury securities of similar maturity to the cash flows of the direct loan or loan guarantee for which the estimate is being made.

children.label: F
children.text: When funds are obligated for a direct loan or loan guarantee, the estimated cost shall be based on the current assumptions, adjusted to incorporate the terms of the loan contract, for the fiscal year in which the funds are obligated.

children.heading: .—Current
children.label: 2 .—Current
children.text: The term “current” has the same meaning as in section 250(c)(9) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985.

children.heading: .—Direct loan
children.label: 3 .—Direct loan
children.text: The term “direct loan” means a disbursement of funds by the Government to a non-Federal borrower under a contract that requires the repayment of such funds. The term includes the purchase of, or participation in, a loan made by another lender and financing arrangements that defer payment for more than 90 days, including the sale of a Government asset on credit terms. The term does not include the acquisition of a federally guaranteed loan in satisfaction of default claims.

children.heading: .—Direct loan obligation
children.label: 4 .—Direct loan obligation
children.text: The term “direct loan obligation” means a binding agreement by the Secretary to make a direct loan when specified conditions are fulfilled by the borrower.

children.heading: .—Intermodal
children.label: 5 .—Intermodal
children.text: The term “intermodal” means of or relating to the connection between rail service and other modes of transportation, including all parts of facilities at which such connection is made.

children.heading: .—Investment-grade rating
children.label: 6 .—Investment-grade rating
children.text: The term “investment-grade rating” means a rating of BBB minus, Baa 3, bbb minus, BBB(low), or higher assigned by a rating agency.

children.heading: .—Loan guarantee
children.label: 7 .—Loan guarantee
children.text: The term “loan guarantee” means any guarantee, insurance, or other pledge with respect to the payment of all or a part of the principal or interest on any debt obligation of a non-Federal borrower to a non-Federal lender, but does not include the insurance of deposits, shares, or other withdrawable accounts in financial institutions.

children.heading: .—Loan guarantee commitment
children.label: 8 .—Loan guarantee commitment
children.text: The term “loan guarantee commitment” means a binding agreement by the Secretary to make a loan guarantee when specified conditions are fulfilled by the borrower, the lender, or any other party to the guarantee agreement.

children.heading: .—Master credit agreement
children.label: 9 .—Master credit agreement
children.text: The term “master credit agreement” means an agreement to make 1 or more direct loans or loan guarantees at future dates for a program of related projects on terms acceptable to the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Modification
children.label: 10 .—Modification
children.text: The term “modification” means any Government action that alters the estimated cost of an outstanding direct loan (or direct loan obligation) or an outstanding loan guarantee (or loan guarantee commitment) from the current estimate of cash flows. This includes the sale of loan assets, with or without recourse, and the purchase of guaranteed loans. This also includes any action resulting from new legislation, or from the exercise of administrative discretion under existing law, that directly or indirectly alters the estimated cost of outstanding direct loans (or direct loan obligations) or loan guarantees (or loan guarantee commitments) such as a change in collection procedures.

children.heading: .—Project obligation
children.label: 11 .—Project obligation
children.text: The term “project obligation” means a note, bond, debenture, or other debt obligation issued by a borrower in connection with the financing of a project, other than a direct loan or loan guarantee under this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: any railroad or railroad carrier (as such terms are defined in section 20102); and

children.label: B
children.text: any rail carrier (as defined in section 24102).

children.heading: .—Rating agency
children.label: 13 .—Rating agency
children.text: The term “rating agency” means a credit rating agency registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission as a nationally recognized statistical rating organization (as defined in section 3(a) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ()).15 U.S.C. 78c(a)

children.heading: .—Secretary
children.label: 14 .—Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Transportation.

children.label: A
children.text: the opening of a project to passenger or freight traffic; or

children.label: B
children.text: a comparable event, as determined by the Secretary and specified in the terms of the direct loan or loan guarantee provided by the Secretary.
